title: Special characters in proxy env vars not escaped
body:
### Issue Description

When exporting HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables for authenticated proxy, if password contains special characters ('%','\','#','@', ...) podman is not able to escape them, resulting in failed operations.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. password="my\pass*word."
2. export HTTP_PROXY="http://username:$password@myproxy:3128"
3. export HTTPS_PROXY="http://username:$password@myproxy:3128"
4. podman login myregistry.example.com



### Describe the results you received

Error: authenticating creds for "myregistry.example.com": pinging container registry myregistry.example.com: Get "https://myregistry.example.com/v2/": proxyconnect tcp: dial tcp: lookup http on 123.45.78.9:53: no such host

### Describe the results you expected

Username: john_doe
Password:
Login Succeeded!

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.2.2
API Version:  5.2.2
Go Version:   go1.22.12 (Red Hat 1.22.12-1.el9_5)
Built:        Fri Apr 11 08:53:34 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
We are using the standard golang `http.ProxyFromEnvironment` function AFAICT so if this would be problem that most likely with the go std not our code directly.

That said I am not sure special chars are valid there at all. Inside a URL you must percent escape such characters https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986#section-2.2 yourself otherwise it is simply not a valid URL.
